findings.issue: ChatManager.session is private but mutated via `as any` cast in 3 places — breaks encapsulation, bypasses type checks, and may corrupt session state
findings.recommendation: Add public `clearSession()` and `setSession(session)` methods to ChatManager, remove `as any` casts

findings.issue: ChatSession.messages is private but mutated via `as any` cast — breaks encapsulation, bypasses message validation
findings.recommendation: Add a public `restoreMessages(msgs)` method to ChatSession

findings.issue: Global error handler uses string matching on error messages for status codes — fragile, breaks if message text changes
findings.recommendation: Use `instanceof` checks (NotFoundError, ValidationError) or a status property on custom error classes instead of message string matching

findings.issue: TaskService (587 lines) has no dedicated test file — the core CRUD service of the entire application lacks direct unit test coverage
findings.recommendation: Create tests/core/tasks.test.ts with comprehensive coverage of create, update, delete, complete, indent/outdent, relations, and recurrence

findings.issue: Database query layer (348 lines) has no test file — data access layer untested directly
findings.recommendation: Create tests/db/queries.test.ts covering all CRUD operations, edge cases for NULL handling, and concurrent access

findings.issue: ProjectService has no dedicated test file — critical path for project CRUD untested
findings.recommendation: Create tests/core/projects.test.ts

findings.issue: EventBus has no test file — event dispatch reliability is untested, could silently break plugin/AI integrations
findings.recommendation: Create tests/core/event-bus.test.ts covering subscribe, unsubscribe, emit, error isolation

findings.issue: Markdown storage uses `as any` to access TaskRow.sectionId and pass updates — indicates type model mismatch between storage backends
findings.recommendation: Ensure TaskRow type includes sectionId, or create a proper typed update interface for the markdown backend
findings.code_snippet: if ((entry.row as any).sectionId === id) { updateTask(idx, taskId, { sectionId: null } as any); }

findings.issue: Category parameter cast to `as any` when calling updateAiMemory — type mismatch between API and storage layer
findings.recommendation: Define a proper union type for memory categories and use it consistently

findings.issue: Undeclared `projectName` property added to TaskFilter via `as any` — creates a shadow type contract not enforced by TypeScript
findings.recommendation: Add `projectName?: string` to the TaskFilter type definition

findings.issue: Update call uses `as any` to pass `parentId` — suggests UpdateTaskInput type is incomplete
findings.recommendation: Add `parentId` to UpdateTaskInput type if task nesting is a supported feature
findings.code_snippet: return this.update(id, { parentId: prevSibling.id } as any);

findings.issue: Project row construction uses `as any` — the constructed object doesn't match ProjectRow type
findings.recommendation: Ensure all required ProjectRow fields are populated or adjust the type to match what markdown storage actually provides

findings.issue: File has 5 eslint-disable + `as any` usages in 55 lines — high density of type suppressions for a bridge module
findings.recommendation: Define proper types for plugin component resolution (e.g., `PluginComponent = React.FC<Record<string, unknown>>`)
findings.code_snippet: let cachedTimeblockingProxy: any = null; const cachedComponents = new Map<string, (props: any) => any>();

findings.issue: StorePlugin type is missing `permissions` field — accessed via `as any` cast 3 times
findings.recommendation: Add `permissions?: string[]` to the StorePlugin type definition

findings.issue: AI error classification accesses `.status`, `.headers`, `.code` via `as any` — no proper error type narrowing
findings.recommendation: Define an interface for provider error shapes (e.g., `ProviderError { status?: number; code?: string; headers?: Record<string, string> }`) and use type guards
findings.code_snippet: const status = (err as any)?.status; const retryAfter = (err as any)?.headers?.["retry-after"]; const code = (err as any)?.code;

findings.issue: 22 instances of `.catch(() => {})` — silently swallowing errors across settings persistence, AI memory extraction, sound playback, and clipboard operations
findings.recommendation: At minimum log a warning in catch handlers for operations that affect data persistence (settings, AI memories). Fire-and-forget is acceptable for purely cosmetic operations (sounds, clipboard)

findings.issue: Duplicated task extraction logic — identical LIST_PREFIX regex and ACTION_VERBS regex defined independently in both files with comment 'Mirrors the logic in the AI tool'
findings.recommendation: Extract shared constants (LIST_PREFIX, ACTION_VERBS) and the core extraction function to a shared utility module (e.g., src/utils/task-extraction.ts)

findings.issue: AI pipeline core (pipeline.ts, middleware.ts, context.ts, capabilities.ts) has zero test coverage — these handle retry logic, timeouts, and provider capability detection
findings.recommendation: Create tests/ai/core/ test files, especially for middleware chain and retry logic

findings.issue: Markdown storage backend has no per-module tests for task-ops, project-ops, metadata-ops, persistence, types — only integration-level coverage exists
findings.recommendation: Create unit tests for individual markdown operation modules

findings.issue: Four core service modules lack dedicated test files — sections, stats, tags, and templates
findings.recommendation: Create corresponding test files in tests/core/

findings.issue: 46 source files exceed 300 lines — largest is tasks.ts at 587, chat.ts at 512, sqlite-backend.ts at 450
findings.recommendation: Target files >400 lines for decomposition in future sprints. tasks.ts could split task relations and recurrence into separate modules

findings.issue: Test coverage gap: 290 of 415 source files (70%) have no corresponding test file. While integration tests cover some, core modules (tasks, projects, tags, event-bus, db/queries) are critical gaps
findings.recommendation: Prioritize test creation for: core/tasks.ts, core/projects.ts, core/event-bus.ts, db/queries.ts, ai/core/pipeline.ts

findings.issue: 33 total `as any` casts across 19 source files — undermines TypeScript strict mode benefits
findings.recommendation: Systematically replace with proper types, type guards, or generic helpers. Target zero `as any` in core/ and storage/ directories first

findings.issue: ErrorBoundary only wraps the main view area — sidebar, AI chat panel, and task detail panel are unprotected
findings.recommendation: Wrap Sidebar, AIChatPanel, and TaskDetailPanel each in their own ErrorBoundary to prevent a plugin or AI crash from taking down the entire UI

findings.issue: Memory extraction fire-and-forget on session switch could silently lose user memories
findings.recommendation: Log failures from extractMemories() and consider awaiting it with a timeout before switching sessions

findings.issue: HTTP status code cast to `as any` suggests Hono's type for status codes is more restrictive than needed
findings.recommendation: Use Hono's `StatusCode` type or cast to a specific numeric literal union instead of `any`
findings.code_snippet: return c.json({ error: message }, status as any);

findings.issue: Single TODO comment in entry point — minor but indicates incomplete wiring
findings.recommendation: Implement or remove the TODO
findings.code_snippet: // TODO: Start UI or CLI based on context

findings.issue: 4 `as any` casts for piper-tts library — library types may be incomplete
findings.recommendation: Create a local type declaration file (piper-tts.d.ts) with proper types for the library API

findings.issue: Browser Speech Recognition API accessed via `(window as any)` — standard Web Speech API types available via @types/dom-speech-recognition
findings.recommendation: Install `@types/dom-speech-recognition` or add a local `.d.ts` declaration for SpeechRecognition

findings.issue: Settings object accessed via `as any` to read calendar_default_mode — settings type doesn't include this key
findings.recommendation: Add calendar_default_mode to the settings type definition
findings.code_snippet: const defaultMode = (settings as any).calendar_default_mode as CalendarMode | undefined;
